name: Kindergarten Tabletop Game Design — Semester 1
description: An 18-week, play-based Kindergarten pathway for rules, turn-taking, chance, and game materials. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning.
learningGoals: Build confidence and curiosity with rules, turn-taking, chance, and game materials.
Communicate learning through talk, drawing, movement, play, or a simple creation.
Practice safe, kind, and responsible participation.

lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about rules, turn-taking, chance, and game materials through a close look at rules, turn-taking, chance, and game materials.

resources.title: Design and computational-thinking pathway

units.title: Wonder and notice: rules, turn-taking, chance, and game materials
units.essentialQuestion: What can we notice and wonder about rules, turn-taking, chance, and game materials?
outcomes: I can notice details and share a question about rules, turn-taking, chance, and game materials.
I can use words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate my thinking.

lessons.title: Notice and talk: rules, turn-taking, chance, and game materials

activities.title: A close look at rules, turn-taking, chance, and game materials
activities.learnerAction: With an adult or teacher nearby when needed, the learner participates in a close look at rules, turn-taking, chance, and game materials, uses words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate thinking, and cleans up safely.

successCriteria: The learner participates safely and respectfully in rules, turn-taking, chance, and game materials.
The learner communicates one observation, choice, or question using speech, drawing, movement, or a supported response.
